text: Canyonlands National Park
zh: 峡谷地国家公园

text: Vast Spectacular Canyonlands National Park of the Colorado Plateau,
zh: 辽阔壮观的峡谷地国家公园,坐落于科罗拉多高原,

text: the park is located in the southeastern part of Utah, an area rich in canyons.
zh: 公园位于犹他州东南部,一个峡谷密布的地区。

text: At the confluence of the Colorado and Green Rivers, a mesa rises from sheer sandstone stone cliffs a thousand feet into the air,
zh: 在科罗拉多河与格林河交汇处,一座平顶山从千英尺高的陡峭砂岩崖壁上拔地而起,

text: over the surrounding canyonlands panorama, the island in the sky, a 43 square mile wilderness mason.
zh: 俯瞰四周的峡谷全景——这便是『天空之岛』,一片43平方英里的荒野。

text: Rugged.
zh: 崎岖。

text: maze of unexplored canyons.
zh: 一片未经探索的峡谷迷宫。

text: A winding labyrinth of hazardous chasms.
zh: 一座由危险沟壑组成的蜿蜒迷宫。

text: One of the most inaccessible regions in the United States.
zh: 美国最难抵达的地区之一。

text: Cross the Colorado River from the maze and you're in the needles.
zh: 从『迷宫』区渡过科罗拉多河,你就到了『针尖』区。

text: Wilderness.
zh: 荒野。

text: Massive spires of rust and coral standing in twisted formation.
zh: 锈红与珊瑚色的巨大尖峰,扭曲交错地矗立着。

text: Solid rock patterned with red and white bands, which were 270 million years in the making.
zh: 坚硬的岩石带着红白相间的纹路,历经2.7亿年才形成。

text: Search the landforms of Needles and you'll come across ancient Indian ruins
zh: 在『针尖』区的地貌中探寻,你会邂逅古老的印第安遗迹,

text: and these prehistoric petroglyphs and pictographs.
zh: 以及这些史前的岩刻与岩画。

text: Needles is also the site of the Cave Spring Trail.
zh: 『针尖』区也是洞泉步道的所在。

text: Here is where cowboys used to herd their cattle.
zh: 这里曾是牛仔们赶牛的地方。

text: Traces of what had been their camps are found here, in this cave.
zh: 他们营地的痕迹就留在这里,在这个洞穴中。

text: Cowboys no longer come to the cave to get water, but birds do.
zh: 牛仔们不再来洞里取水了,但鸟儿会。

text: Nevertheless, this is cowboy country.
zh: 尽管如此,这里仍是牛仔的天下。

text: Cowboys still tend cattle and acreage skirting the park.
zh: 牛仔们依旧在公园边缘放牧、耕作。

text: Just outside the park is Robbers Roost, where the infamous Wild Bunch hit out.
zh: 公园外不远处就是『强盗巢穴』,臭名昭著的『野帮伙』当年就在那里出没。

text: The Roost's most celebrated outlaws are Butch Cassidy and the Sundance Kid, hit, best known as they were portrayed in a classic Hollywood western.
zh: 巢穴中最有名的亡命徒是布奇·卡西迪和圣丹斯小子,他们因一部经典好莱坞西部片的刻画而广为人知。

text: Those runaway outlaws have long since fled, but the bighorn sheep are still here, though very wary.
zh: 那些逃亡的亡命徒早已远去,但大角羊仍在这里,只是十分警觉。

text: Their skittishness is understandable.
zh: 它们的胆怯是可以理解的。

text: Before park protection, they'd been hunted almost to extinction.
zh: 在公园设立保护之前,它们曾被猎杀到近乎灭绝。

text: It's simply amazing, their ability to skitter on the narrowest of ledges at the highest of heights.
zh: 它们能在最高处最窄的岩架上疾走,实在令人惊叹。

text: While hiking, should you stumble on remote Horseshoe Canyon, you'll encounter a great gallery of prehistoric rock art.
zh: 徒步时,你若偶然闯入偏远的马蹄峡谷,会遇见一处宏大的史前岩画长廊,

text: one of the most significant in North America.
zh: 堪称北美最重要的岩画之一。

text: Sinister, faceless, bigger -than -life figures hovering over you.
zh: 阴森、无面、比真人还大的身影,悬在你的头顶。

text: Taller than humans, there are those who believe that they are pictures of one -time visitors from outer space.
zh: 它们比人还高;有人相信,这些是昔日外太空来客的画像。

text: More likely, ritual rock art creations of nomadic archaic people who lived in these canyons several thousand years ago.
zh: 更可能的是,几千年前生活在这些峡谷中的游牧古人所作的仪式岩画。

text: Where the two rivers collide, the Colorado and the Green, the rivers that carve these canyons, they're transformed into raging whitewater.
zh: 在科罗拉多河与格林河这两条雕凿峡谷的河流相撞之处,它们化作汹涌的激流。

text: Rafters need permits to run the course, with a skilled river runner in charge.
zh: 漂流者需持许可证、由熟练的漂流向导带队,才能闯这段水路。

text: Here, the waters plunge 30 feet in just one mile.
zh: 在这里,河水在短短一英里内骤降30英尺。

text: A Jeep road over Elephant Hill is the most challenging trailway in the park.
zh: 翻越象山的吉普车道,是公园里最具挑战的路线。

text: You need a four -wheel drive vehicle to climb it.
zh: 你需要一辆四驱车才能爬上去。

text: you'll see plenty of tricked -out cars chugging noisily up this rock -strewn slope.
zh: 你会看到不少改装车轰鸣着,吃力地爬上这条乱石遍布的坡道。

text: A sportier and increasingly popular way to negotiate these tortuous dirt roads is mountain biking.
zh: 一种更具运动感、也越来越流行的方式,是骑山地车走这些崎岖土路。

text: Stand on a park overlook and gasp at the spectacle before you.
zh: 站在公园的观景点上,为眼前的壮景倒吸一口气。

text: Canyon after canyon reaching to the horizon.
zh: 一道又一道峡谷,一直延伸到地平线。

text: This isn't simply the Wild West.
zh: 这不只是狂野西部。

text: It's America at its wildest.
zh: 这是美国最狂野的一面。
